Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired, as indicated by elevated creatinine. Mild dyslipidemia is present (high total cholesterol and triglycerides), but HDL is within a protective range. Low globulin and alkaline phosphatase may warrant further evaluation but are not immediately concerning. Other parameters, including glucose, electrolytes, liver enzymes, and albumin, are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation and assessment of renal function and cardiovascular risk.
